title: Isoloma longiflora

Within the Hymenophyllaceae family, Isoloma longiflora is further classified as being part of the genus Isoloma. The genus Isoloma is a group of epiphytic ferns that are native to the tropical regions of the world. Isoloma longiflora is a species within this genus and is known for its unique characteristics and adaptations.

I couldn't find any information about Isoloma longiflora. However, I can provide information on Isoloma, which is a genus of lichenized fungi. 

Isoloma is a genus of lichenized fungi, specifically a type of fungus that grows on trees or rocks, often forming crust-like or leaf-like structures. It is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Isoloma may include compounds such as phenolic acids, terpenoids, and flavonoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Isoloma are not well-documented, but it is often used in traditional medicine to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that may be used to treat various health conditions.
